module_boundaries.responsibility: shared game types, versioning, deterministic ids, manifest schemas, and production data contracts
custom C++ live-water bridge, fixed-step stepping, report-manifest loading, water samples, and solver telemetry
river corridors, source manifests, coordinate transforms, reach-local grids, stitched validation outputs, and Unreal corridor packages
selected raft/contact authority path, raft actors, patch sampling, contacts, and replayable physics telemetry
crew safety state, swimming skill policy, command response, swimmer/rescue state, local AI voice hooks, and passenger personas
Enhanced Input actions, VR/gamepad/keyboard mappings, command intents, and accessibility fallbacks
interactive water audio, raft/crew cues, spatial presets, audio source manifests, and runtime audio telemetry
menus, river selection, replay, scoring, settings, and player-facing debug view models
physics, water, river, audio, AI, replay, validation, and runtime-budget overlays
Unreal automation manifests, regression fixture imports, live-water smoke tests, and accepted-report evidence checks
future multiplayer sessions, replication, latency prediction, spatial voice, scoring contracts, and network telemetry

production_rules: RaftSimWater remains authoritative for live water and consumes the Milestone 20 report-set lock until a newer accepted lock supersedes it.
RaftSimPhysics owns raft/contact authority only through the selected runtime path recorded by Milestone 19.
RaftSimGeo and RaftSimRiver must preserve source provenance, CRS metadata, reach-local ghost-zone metadata, and stitched whole-window validation outputs.
RaftSimAutomation must compare Unreal live/replayed fields against accepted C++ outputs before a milestone gate can close.
Editor-generated binary assets stay out of source control until intentionally created and reviewed.
